title: Propeller Falling from an Airplane
body: An aircraft is coming in for a landing at $$300$$ meters height when the propeller falls off. The aircraft is flying at $$40.0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ horizontally. The propeller has a rotation rate of $$20$$ $$\frac{rev}{s}$$, a moment of inertia of $$70.0$$ $$kg m^2$$, and a mass of $$200$$ kg. Neglect air resistance so the rotation rate does not change as the propeller falls. Use $$g=9.8$$ $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$.
